pattern: Running sums of the primes 2, 2+3, 2+3+5, ... grow strictly, so once that running total passes n every later candidate can be skipped, leaving only a short prefix of primes to check for primality of both the prime itself and its accumulated sum.
hints: Sieve every prime up to n once, then walk the primes in increasing order while keeping a running sum.
Stop accumulating as soon as the running sum exceeds n -- the sum only grows, so nothing useful can appear afterward.

Whenever the running sum is at most n, check whether that sum itself is prime (reuse the sieve) and keep the largest one you find; a sum that lands exactly on a non-prime number must be discarded in favor of the last valid one.

edge_inputs.description: smallest possible input, below the first prime's sum
n equal to the first prime itself
n just above the first prime, no room for a second term
n exactly equal to the two-term sum 2+3
statement-style case with a few extra primes available
the running sum lands exactly on n but that sum is not itself prime, so the previous valid sum must be kept
the running sum lands exactly on n and n itself is prime, so it becomes the new best

stress.description: moderate n values across the valid range
n values near the maximum allowed bound
